content:
As a Software Engineer at Microsoft and someone who is building AI-powered SaaS, I've noticed a pattern:

Code generation is getting faster every month. Code review isn't.
And that's becoming the real bottleneck.

• Understanding the overall change
• Identifying risky modifications
• Verifying logic across multiple files
• Spotting potential bugs and edge cases

The larger the codebase gets, the more expensive this process becomes!

That's why I found CodeRabbit Review UI interesting.

Instead of scrolling through endless file diffs and comments, the review experience is designed around understanding changes faster and acting on them efficiently.

Here's what stood out to me:

✅ AI-generated review summaries
Instead of manually piecing together what changed, you get a high-level overview of the pull request upfront.

✅ Context-aware review comments
The feedback isn't limited to syntax or style. It helps surface issues that could impact maintainability and code quality.

✅ Faster navigation through PRs
The interface makes it easier to move through changes and focus attention where it matters most.

✅ Built for modern AI-assisted development
As engineers increasingly use AI to write code, having AI help review code feels like the natural next step.

The future isn't AI replacing developers.
It's developers using AI to remove repetitive work and spend more time on engineering decisions that actually matter.

Code generation was step one. Code review is the next.

Try CodeRabbit for code reviews: https://lnkd.in/gY7eMTCX

content:
As a Software Engineer at Microsoft and someone who is building AI-powered SaaS, I've noticed a pattern:

Code generation is getting faster every month. Code review isn't.
And that's becoming the real bottleneck.

• Understanding the overall change
• Identifying risky modifications
• Verifying logic across multiple files
• Spotting potential bugs and edge cases

The larger the codebase gets, the more expensive this process becomes!

That's why I found CodeRabbit Review UI interesting.

Instead of scrolling through endless file diffs and comments, the review experience is designed around understanding changes faster and acting on them efficiently.

Here's what stood out to me:

✅ AI-generated review summaries
Instead of manually piecing together what changed, you get a high-level overview of the pull request upfront.

✅ Context-aware review comments
The feedback isn't limited to syntax or style. It helps surface issues that could impact maintainability and code quality.

✅ Faster navigation through PRs
The interface makes it easier to move through changes and focus attention where it matters most.

✅ Built for modern AI-assisted development
As engineers increasingly use AI to write code, having AI help review code feels like the natural next step.

The future isn't AI replacing developers.
It's developers using AI to remove repetitive work and spend more time on engineering decisions that actually matter.

Code generation was step one. Code review is the next.

Try CodeRabbit for code reviews: https://lnkd.in/gY7eMTCX

#ai #code #sde #codereview #agents

content:
2022 → Intuit (internship), struggled to clear OAs
2023 → Failed my Blinkit interview due to DSA
2024 → Cracked Cisco (off-campus) - graduated
2026 → Switched to Microsoft. Offers from Amazon and other PBCs.

My journey wasn’t smooth. Mainly because:

❌ I practiced DSA but never identified my weak areas.
I kept solving problems without tracking patterns I failed in.

❌ I built a resume but didn't optimize it.

content:
Senior developers are losing opportunities to Gen-AI Engineers. Know why?
A new role has appeared on every tech company's hiring page in 2026.

Gen-AI Engineer.

Average salary? 30 to 50% higher than a senior developer.
Years of experience? Skills matter more than tenure now.

Here's what they're actually looking for:

1. Devs who can BUILD AI features.
Not "use ChatGPT to code faster." Build the actual chatbot, search agent, or RAG system the company ships.

2. Production-grade skills.
RAG pipelines. AI agents. MCP servers. AWS Bedrock deployment. Not theory. Real working systems.

3. Speed over seniority.
A junior who can ship a working agent in 2 weeks is more valuable than a senior still calling AI "just hype."

Here's the hardest pill to swallow:

Senior developers who don't build Gen-AI systems are being passed over for juniors who can.
Not because experience doesn't matter. But because building Gen-AI is a different skill. And companies need it now.

The real question for 2026 isn't "Will AI replace me?"

It's "Can I build the AI features my company needs?"
